title: Dig into Creative Europe’s achievements through stats and data visualisations
content:
Dig into Creative Europe’s achievements through stats and data visualisations

csudaga Wed, 04/08/2026 - 12:46

How many projects has Creative Europe supported in past years? In which cultural and creative sectors were they active? How many grantees has Culture Moves Europe supported in recent years? Where do grantees travel to and from, around Europe and beyond?

Up-to-date, interactive data on Creative Europe

These data and more are now available in interactive overview, providing access to the programme's performance metrics and data to anyone interested. The accompanying page “Creative Europe in numbers” guides you through the dashboard and offers ready-made dynamic charts to get you initiated into the wealth of the programme data.

With this tool the European Commission aims to foster transparency and deepen the understanding about Creative Europe's activities and accomplishments through data

title: European Heritage Label: 2027 selection procedure now open
content:
European Heritage Label: 2027 selection procedure now open

csudaga Thu, 04/09/2026 - 15:06

The selection procedure for the 2027 edition of the European Heritage Label (EHL) action is open. Interested organisations should contact the EHL coordinators in their respective countries for the details of the application, as this first stage of the process is done at national level.

Intro Text

Heritage organisations are invited to apply for the call. The initial procedure is carried out at national level.

title: New study recommends improvements for the discoverability of European cultural content online
content:
New study recommends improvements for the discoverability of European cultural content online

csudaga Tue, 03/31/2026 - 16:03

A new study prepared for the European Commission details how discoverability has become the critical factor determining which cultural works can rise above the digital noise and be noticed, accessed, and engaged with by audiences.

The research, commissioned under the EU Work Plan for Culture 2023–2026, examines how European cultural content is discovered and accessed in today’s digital environment.  The study introduces a new conceptual framework for understanding discoverability in the digital era, where interconnected elements work together to determine which works reach audiences and which remain hidden.

A focus on music and books

It provides detailed analysis of the music and book sectors, with insights into other cultural and creative sectors and industries affected by the increasing use of platforms and algorithms. The study analyses how di

title: Creative Europe at the 2026 Brussels book fair
content:
Creative Europe at the 2026 Brussels book fair

csudaga Fri, 03/20/2026 - 14:25

Highlights of the fair

Inaugural ceremony

European Commissioner Glenn Micallef will speak at the opening of the event on 25 March, showcasing the EU's commitment to supporting the book industry and promoting reading.

Support for Ukraine

Commissioner Micallef will also participate in the distribution of Creative Europe-funded books to Ukrainian children.

Engaging discussions

Meet authors, translators, publishers, booksellers, and librarians to explore topics like literary translation, cross-border outreach, digital innovation, and tackling disinformation. Most events will be held in French.

Practical information

Visit us at Gare Maritime’s Place à l’Europe, stands 148–152, throughout the fair. Attendance is free, but registration is required.

Intro Text

title: Overview of Culture Moves Europe mobilities
content:
Overview of Culture Moves Europe mobilities

csudaga Fri, 03/20/2026 - 10:39
This page presents information about mobility projects funded through the Culture Moves Europe action of Creative Europe – Culture strand. It includes an interactive dashboard, data visualisations and maps.

Started in 2022, Culture Moves Europe supports individual mobility and residency projects across Creative Europe countries, territories and regions. It gives grants to artists and cultural professionals to travel and carry out a project in another Creative Europe country.

Mobilities per sector
